purpose: Primary drive component that converts electrical voltage commands into mechanical spindle rotation. Governs speed, torque, and current delivery to the spindle. Subject to thermal accumulation, velocity control degradation, and position accuracy loss, all of which directly impact machining precision.

inputs.name: motor_voltage_command
inputs.description: Commanded voltage level sent to the motor drive, expressed as a percentage of full-scale voltage. Primary control input driving speed and torque output.

outputs.name: actual_spindle_speed
outputs.description: Measured rotational speed of the spindle shaft. Degraded by velocity control errors and bearing wear.

outputs.name: zero_speed_detection
outputs.description: True when spindle speed is at or below the zero-speed threshold, indicating the motor has stopped or nearly stopped.

outputs.name: motor_current
outputs.description: Instantaneous motor phase current draw. Increases under overload, bearing degradation, or motor lock conditions.

outputs.name: drive_torque
outputs.description: Mechanical torque delivered to the spindle. Reduced by bearing wear and grease degradation.

states.name: motor_temperature
states.description: Thermal energy accumulated in the motor windings and housing. Rises with load (voltage command) and dissipates toward ambient when unloaded. Bounded by ambient floor and physical destruction ceiling.

states.name: velocity_error_accumulation
states.description: Accumulated deviation between commanded and actual spindle speed. Builds when the drive cannot track the command accurately due to degradation or overload. Bounded by a maximum observable error limit.

states.name: position_accuracy_degradation
states.degradation_timescale: ~33 operating cycles (derived from bearing_wear_rate=0.02 as proxy; no direct simulator rate provided — see assumptions)
states.description: Normalized health index for encoder/coder signal fidelity and position feedback accuracy. Starts at 1.0 (perfect) and degrades toward 0.0 as encoder wear and signal noise accumulate. Drives position error growth.

states.name: bearing_wear
states.degradation_timescale: ~50 operating cycles at rate 0.02 per cycle
states.description: Normalized bearing health index. Starts at 1.0 (new) and degrades toward 0.0 as rolling element fatigue and surface wear accumulate under load and speed. Directly increases vibration and current draw.

states.name: grease_degradation
states.degradation_timescale: ~33 operating cycles at rate 0.03 per cycle
states.description: Normalized lubricant health index for spindle motor bearings. Degrades due to thermal stress and oxidation. Loss of lubrication increases friction, heat, and bearing wear rate.

states.name: preload_degradation
states.degradation_timescale: ~67 operating cycles at rate 0.015 per cycle
states.description: Normalized bearing preload health. Degrades through thermal cycling and mechanical wear, reducing stiffness and increasing runout, which degrades position accuracy and increases vibration.

states.name: contamination_level
states.degradation_timescale: ~100 operating cycles at rate 0.01 per cycle
states.description: Normalized cleanliness index of the motor/bearing cavity. Starts at 1.0 (clean) and degrades as metallic wear particles accumulate. Contamination accelerates bearing and grease degradation.

states.name: actual_speed_tracked
states.description: Internal first-order lag state tracking the commanded spindle speed. Used to compute zero_speed_detection and overspeed event thresholds without referencing raw inputs directly in event logic.

events.name: motor_overheat
events.description: Motor winding temperature exceeds the overheat alarm threshold (140 °C). Triggers protective shutdown to prevent insulation damage. event_state = 1.0 when healthy (temperature low), transitions toward 0.0 as temperature rises, crosses 0.0 at threshold.

events.name: excessive_velocity_error
events.description: Accumulated velocity error exceeds the allowable limit, indicating the drive cannot maintain commanded speed. Driven by position accuracy degradation and velocity error buildup. event_state = 1.0 when healthy.

events.name: overspeed
events.description: Spindle speed exceeds the maximum rated speed threshold, risking mechanical damage. event_state = 1.0 when speed is within safe range, crosses 0.0 when tracked speed exceeds overspeed limit.

events.name: short_time_overload
events.description: Motor subjected to short-duration overload current beyond rated capacity. Indicated by elevated temperature and degraded bearing health increasing friction load. event_state = 1.0 when healthy.

events.name: motor_lock
events.description: Motor rotor is mechanically locked or stalled, typically due to severe bearing seizure from combined bearing wear, contamination, and lubrication failure. event_state = 1.0 when healthy, approaches 0.0 as all three degradation states deteriorate.

events.name: current_overload
events.description: Motor current exceeds the continuous rated current limit. Caused by increased mechanical resistance from bearing wear and contamination, reflected in rising temperature. event_state = 1.0 when healthy.

parameters.name: motor_thermal_gain
parameters.description: Rate of temperature rise per unit voltage command per unit time. Represents motor thermal resistance and heat generation coefficient.

parameters.name: motor_thermal_dissipation
parameters.description: Rate of temperature decay toward ambient when unloaded. Represents motor thermal capacitance and convective cooling.

parameters.name: ambient_temperature
parameters.description: Ambient reference temperature to which the motor cools when unloaded.

parameters.name: overheat_threshold
parameters.description: Motor temperature alarm threshold. Triggers motor_overheat event when exceeded.

parameters.name: critical_temperature
parameters.description: Absolute maximum motor temperature before catastrophic insulation failure.

parameters.name: velocity_error_buildup_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which velocity error accumulates per unit voltage command when position accuracy is degraded.

parameters.name: velocity_error_decay_rate
parameters.description: Natural decay rate of velocity error accumulation when drive is healthy.

parameters.name: max_velocity_error
parameters.description: Maximum allowable accumulated velocity error before excessive_velocity_error event triggers.

parameters.name: critical_position_error
parameters.description: Critical position error threshold in encoder counts equivalent, mapped to velocity error accumulation for event logic.

parameters.name: bearing_wear_rate
parameters.description: Rate of bearing health degradation per unit driver activity per unit time. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: critical_bearing_wear
parameters.description: Bearing wear health index below which motor_lock and current_overload events are triggered. From simulator ground truth (health = 1 - wear fraction, so threshold health = 1 - 0.8 = 0.2).

parameters.name: preload_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate of bearing preload health degradation per unit driver activity per unit time. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: grease_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate of lubricant health degradation per unit driver activity per unit time. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: contamination_buildup_rate
parameters.description: Rate of contamination index degradation per unit driver activity per unit time. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: position_accuracy_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate of position accuracy health degradation per unit driver activity. Derived from bearing_wear_rate as proxy (no direct simulator rate provided).

parameters.name: speed_scale_factor
parameters.description: Conversion factor from voltage command percentage to target spindle speed in rpm. Maps 100% command to maximum rated speed.

parameters.name: speed_tracking_coefficient
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for actual_speed_tracked approaching target speed. Capped at 0.5 for numerical stability.

parameters.name: zero_speed_threshold
parameters.description: Speed below which zero_speed_detection is reported as true. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: overspeed_threshold
parameters.description: Speed above which the overspeed alarm is triggered.

parameters.name: speed_arrival_threshold
parameters.description: Speed error margin within which the motor is considered to have arrived at commanded speed. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: nominal_current_scale
parameters.description: Nominal motor current at 100% voltage command under healthy conditions.

parameters.name: current_overload_threshold
parameters.description: Motor current above which current_overload event is triggered.

parameters.name: nominal_torque_scale
parameters.description: Nominal drive torque at 100% voltage command under healthy bearing and lubrication conditions.

parameters.name: short_time_overload_temp_threshold
parameters.description: Temperature threshold for short_time_overload warning, set below the hard overheat alarm.

parameters.name: short_time_overload_bearing_threshold
parameters.description: Bearing health index below which short_time_overload warning is also considered active.

parameters.name: motor_lock_combined_threshold
parameters.description: Combined degradation threshold: motor_lock triggers when bearing_wear health AND contamination_level AND grease_degradation all fall below this value.

state_transition_logic: motor_temperature: Increase by motor_thermal_gain * (motor_voltage_command / 100.0) * dt to model resistive heating under load. Decrease by motor_thermal_dissipation * (motor_temperature - ambient_temperature) * dt to model convective cooling. Net: motor_temperature += [motor_thermal_gain * (motor_voltage_command / 100.0) - motor_thermal_dissipation * (motor_temperature - ambient_temperature)] * dt. Clamp to [25.0, 200.0].

velocity_error_accumulation: Increase by velocity_error_buildup_rate * (motor_voltage_command / 100.0) * (1.0 - position_accuracy_degradation) * dt, representing error growth when position feedback is degraded. Decrease by velocity_error_decay_rate * velocity_error_accumulation * dt as the drive self-corrects. Net: velocity_error_accumulation += [velocity_error_buildup_rate * (motor_voltage_command / 100.0) * (1.0 - position_accuracy_degradation) - velocity_error_decay_rate * velocity_error_accumulation] * dt. Clamp to [0.0, 5000.0].

position_accuracy_degradation: Decrease by position_accuracy_degradation_rate * load_cycles_and_speed * dt, where load_cycles_and_speed is a normalized driver signal derived from motor_voltage_command. position_accuracy_degradation -= position_accuracy_degradation_rate * (motor_voltage_command / 100.0) * dt. Clamp to [0.0, 1.0].
bearing_wear: Decrease by bearing_wear_rate * load_cycles_and_speed * dt. bearing_wear -= bearing_wear_rate * (motor_voltage_command / 100.0) * dt. Clamp to [0.0, 1.0].

grease_degradation: Decrease by grease_degradation_rate * thermal_stress_and_oxidation * dt, where thermal_stress_and_oxidation is proxied by normalized motor_temperature above ambient. grease_degradation -= grease_degradation_rate * ((motor_temperature - ambient_temperature) / (critical_temperature - ambient_temperature)) * dt. Clamp to [0.0, 1.0].

preload_degradation: Decrease by preload_degradation_rate * thermal_cycling_and_wear * dt, where thermal_cycling_and_wear is proxied by the product of normalized temperature and normalized voltage command. preload_degradation -= preload_degradation_rate * ((motor_temperature - ambient_temperature) / (critical_temperature - ambient_temperature)) * (motor_voltage_command / 100.0) * dt. Clamp to [0.0, 1.0].

contamination_level: Decrease by contamination_buildup_rate * metallic_wear_particles * dt, where metallic_wear_particles is proxied by (1.0 - bearing_wear), representing particle generation rate increasing as bearing degrades. contamination_level -= contamination_buildup_rate * (1.0 - bearing_wear) * dt. Clamp to [0.0, 1.0].

actual_speed_tracked: Compute target speed as motor_voltage_command * speed_scale_factor. Approach target using first-order lag: actual_speed_tracked += speed_tracking_coefficient * (target_speed - actual_speed_tracked). Coefficient is capped at 0.5 for numerical stability. Clamp to [0.0, 32767.0].
output_logic: actual_spindle_speed: Equal to actual_speed_tracked, further reduced by velocity error and position accuracy degradation. actual_spindle_speed = actual_speed_tracked * position_accuracy_degradation - velocity_error_accumulation * (1.0 - position_accuracy_degradation). Clamp to [0.0, 32767.0]. When position_accuracy_degradation = 1.0 and velocity_error_accumulation = 0.0, output equals actual_speed_tracked exactly.
zero_speed_detection: True when actual_spindle_speed <= zero_speed_threshold, False otherwise. zero_speed_detection = (actual_spindle_speed <= zero_speed_threshold).

motor_current: Nominal current scales with voltage command. Degradation increases current draw due to friction. motor_current = (motor_voltage_command / 100.0) * nominal_current_scale * (1.0 + (1.0 - bearing_wear) * 0.5 + (1.0 - grease_degradation) * 0.3 + (1.0 - contamination_level) * 0.2). Clamp to [0.0, 100.0]. Healthy state (all degradation = 1.0) yields motor_current = (motor_voltage_command / 100.0) * nominal_current_scale.

drive_torque: Nominal torque scales with voltage command. Bearing wear and grease degradation reduce effective torque delivery. drive_torque = (motor_voltage_command / 100.0) * nominal_torque_scale * bearing_wear * grease_degradation * preload_degradation. Healthy state yields drive_torque = (motor_voltage_command / 100.0) * nominal_torque_scale.
event_logic: motor_overheat (SP0001): event_state = (overheat_threshold - motor_temperature) / overheat_threshold. Equals 1.0 when motor_temperature = 0 (healthy/cold), equals 0.0 when motor_temperature = overheat_threshold (140.0 °C). Event triggers when event_state <= 0.0.

excessive_velocity_error (SP0002): event_state = 1.0 - (velocity_error_accumulation / critical_position_error) * (1.0 - position_accuracy_degradation + 0.01). Equals 1.0 when velocity_error_accumulation = 0.0 and position_accuracy_degradation = 1.0. Event triggers when event_state <= 0.0, i.e., when accumulated error scaled by accuracy loss reaches critical_position_error.

overspeed (SP0007): event_state = (overspeed_threshold - actual_speed_tracked) / overspeed_threshold. Equals 1.0 when actual_speed_tracked = 0.0 (healthy/stopped). Event triggers when event_state <= 0.0, i.e., actual_speed_tracked >= overspeed_threshold (32767.0 rpm).

short_time_overload (SP0029): event_state = min((short_time_overload_temp_threshold - motor_temperature) / short_time_overload_temp_threshold, (bearing_wear - short_time_overload_bearing_threshold) / (1.0 - short_time_overload_bearing_threshold)). Equals 1.0 when motor_temperature = 0 and bearing_wear = 1.0. Event triggers when event_state <= 0.0.

motor_lock (SP0031): event_state = min((bearing_wear - motor_lock_combined_threshold) / (1.0 - motor_lock_combined_threshold), (contamination_level - motor_lock_combined_threshold) / (1.0 - motor_lock_combined_threshold), (grease_degradation - motor_lock_combined_threshold) / (1.0 - motor_lock_combined_threshold)). Equals 1.0 when all three health states = 1.0. Event triggers when event_state <= 0.0, meaning all three degradation states have fallen to or below motor_lock_combined_threshold simultaneously.

current_overload (SP0054): event_state = (current_overload_threshold - motor_current) / current_overload_threshold. Equals 1.0 when motor_current = 0.0. Event triggers when event_state <= 0.0, i.e., motor_current >= current_overload_threshold (90.0 A). Motor current is driven by bearing_wear, contamination_level, and motor_temperature via output logic.
assumptions: position_accuracy_degradation_rate (0.02) is derived from bearing_wear_rate as a proxy because no direct simulator rate was provided for encoder/coder signal degradation. This is flagged for calibration against real encoder wear data.

load_cycles_and_speed driver for bearing_wear and position_accuracy_degradation is proxied by normalized motor_voltage_command (motor_voltage_command / 100.0), as no explicit cycle counter input is available in the port definition.

thermal_stress_and_oxidation driver for grease_degradation is proxied by normalized motor temperature above ambient: (motor_temperature - ambient_temperature) / (critical_temperature - ambient_temperature).
thermal_cycling_and_wear driver for preload_degradation is proxied by the product of normalized temperature and normalized voltage command, capturing both thermal and mechanical cycling.
metallic_wear_particles driver for contamination_level is proxied by (1.0 - bearing_wear), representing increasing particle generation as bearing health declines.

critical_bearing_wear from the simulator is 0.8 (fraction worn). In the health-index convention used here (1.0 = new, 0.0 = failed), this corresponds to a health threshold of 1.0 - 0.8 = 0.2, which is used as critical_bearing_wear parameter default.

overspeed_threshold is set to 32767.0 rpm matching the maximum typical output value from the port definition. A more precise mechanical overspeed limit should be substituted when available from the machine datasheet.

motor_thermal_gain (0.5 °C/(%·s)) and motor_thermal_dissipation (0.05 1/s) are physically motivated initial estimates. They should be identified from thermal step-response data or FEM analysis of the specific motor.
speed_scale_factor (327.67 rpm/%) maps 100% command to 32767 rpm maximum speed. This is a linear approximation; actual motor speed-voltage curves may be nonlinear.

The short_time_overload event uses a combined temperature and bearing health criterion because the fault code SP0029 implies transient overload that is exacerbated by both thermal state and mechanical friction increase.

motor_lock event logic requires all three degradation states (bearing_wear, contamination_level, grease_degradation) to simultaneously fall below motor_lock_combined_threshold, reflecting that seizure requires concurrent lubrication failure, contamination, and bearing damage.

velocity_error_buildup_rate (0.5 rpm/(%·s)) and velocity_error_decay_rate (0.1 1/s) are initial estimates not covered by simulator ground truth. They should be calibrated from servo drive response data.

All event_state formulas are constructed so that with healthy initial states (all degradation = 1.0, motor_temperature = 25.0, velocity_error_accumulation = 0.0, actual_speed_tracked = 0.0) and zero or minimal voltage command, all event_state values equal 1.0 as required.
